mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive returns occur in less than 45 percent of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a quarter signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust demand without overheating, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3 percent annually alongside RSAFS expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals improving corporate output capacity and earnings momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of recovery-phase observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect increased construction activity and household confidence, transmitting positive earnings expectations to equity markets over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward returns across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4 percent monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in more than 50 percent of months following CPIAUCSL spikes above the 0.4 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and real GDP confirms synchronized economic expansion, reinforcing corporate revenue forecasts and driving positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and GDP growth signal predicts negative returns in more than 55 percent of synchronized expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals weakening manufacturing demand and lower corporate earnings, generating negative quarterly equity returns during monetary tightening cycles.
failure_condition: Negative return prediction fails when SPX advances in more than 60 percent of observations during tightening cycles.
